sections: How Our Road Freight Service to & from Europe Works

The backbone of any reliable European removal is road freight, and it’s the method we trust for the vast majority of household moves between the UK and the continent. As a family-run Sussex removals & storage company founded in 2016, we’ve refined a process that keeps your belongings on a single, carefully planned journey rather than passing them through a chain of unfamiliar hands. Our vehicles collect from your home across West Sussex, East Sussex, Surrey, Hampshire and Kent, then travel by road and ferry or tunnel crossing to your destination address overseas. Because the same crew that loads in the UK travels with the consignment, accountability stays with one team from your front door to your new home abroad.

Road freight suits the realities of European moving extremely well. It allows us to handle furniture, white goods, antiques and the everyday contents of a family home without the rigid size and weight constraints that catch people out with other methods. It also gives us flexibility on timing, so we can build a schedule around your completion date, tenancy start or the day the keys change hands. If you’re weighing up your options for a move further afield, our international removals service covers the same standards on longer routes, while a friendly chat through the get a quote form is usually the quickest way to confirm the best approach for your particular relocation.
Dedicated Loads vs Part Loads — Choosing the Right Option

One of the first decisions on any European move is whether you need a dedicated load or whether a part load makes more sense. The right choice depends on the volume you’re sending, your budget and how flexible you can be on dates. We’ll talk both through honestly during your survey so you only pay for what genuinely suits your circumstances.

A dedicated load means a vehicle is reserved exclusively for your belongings. It travels directly to your destination, which gives you the tightest possible delivery window and the reassurance that nothing else shares the space. A part load, by contrast, shares vehicle space with other consignments heading in the same direction, which keeps the cost lower for smaller volumes — ideal if you’re moving a one- or two-bedroom property, a part-furnished apartment or a collection of items rather than a whole house.

  • Dedicated loads suit larger family homes, time-critical completions and anyone who wants the most precise delivery date possible.
  • Part loads suit smaller volumes, students, downsizers and second-home owners where a slightly wider delivery window is acceptable.
  • Both options carry the same care, the same trained crews and the same full insurance cover.
  • We’ll recommend the option that gives you the best value for your specific volume rather than simply the largest vehicle.

If you’re unsure which way to go, our transparent pricing approach means you’ll always understand exactly what you’re paying for before you commit.

European removals reward early planning, and the single most useful thing you can do is talk to us as soon as your dates start to firm up. A typical move begins with a survey — in person or by video — so we can assess volume, access at both ends, and any items that need special handling such as pianos, marble tops or fragile antiques. From there we build a written plan covering collection, transit and delivery, along with guidance on the paperwork involved.

Transit timelines vary with distance, crossing availability and whether you’ve chosen a dedicated or part load. Dedicated loads naturally offer the tightest windows because the vehicle travels straight to you; part loads are scheduled around a shared route, so we agree a realistic delivery window in advance rather than promising a single fixed hour. We always build in sensible contingency for ferry timetables, driving-hours regulations and seasonal demand, which tends to peak over summer and around month-end completions. Booking ahead secures both your preferred dates and the right vehicle. The earlier you’re in touch through our request a quote page, the more flexibility we have to fit your move into the calendar smoothly.
Export Packing, Inventories & the Paperwork We Help With

Packing for a European move is a different discipline from a local one. Items endure longer in transit, more handling at crossings, and changes in temperature and humidity, so robust export-grade protection matters. Our export packing service uses heavy-duty materials designed for long journeys, and for high-value or awkward pieces our custom crate service builds bespoke timber crates that shield fragile and valuable items from knocks and pressure. If you’d rather not lift a finger, our full packing service wraps and boxes your entire home to the same standard.

A detailed inventory underpins every international consignment. We record what travels, which supports both your insurance and the customs documentation that accompanies any move across a border. Since the UK left the EU, household goods moving to and from Europe involve more administrative steps than they once did, and we guide you through the documentation in plain English so nothing is overlooked. We keep this guidance general and practical rather than offering country-specific rulings — for definitive customs requirements we’ll always point you to the appropriate authorities — but our experience means the forms, inventories and supporting paperwork are prepared properly and on time.

  • Export-grade materials chosen for long road journeys and multiple handling points.
  • A clear, itemised inventory to support insurance and customs paperwork.
  • Plain-English guidance on the documents a cross-border move typically needs.
  • Custom crating for antiques, art and anything that warrants extra protection.
Delivery, Unpacking & Storage at Either End

Arriving at your new home abroad should feel like the end of the stress, not the start of another job. When the vehicle reaches your destination, our crew places furniture in the rooms you choose, reassembles items we dismantled at collection, and can unpack boxes and remove the packing debris so you’re not left wading through cardboard. The level of service is entirely up to you — some clients want everything unpacked and put away, others prefer to take it from the doorstep.

Timing doesn’t always line up perfectly, and that’s where storage becomes invaluable. If your new property isn’t ready, or you’re moving out before you can move in, our secure storage facility bridges the gap at the UK end so your belongings stay safe until the moment is right. We can also arrange holding solutions at the destination through trusted partners where needed, so a gap in dates never forces a rushed or risky decision. As a LAPADA member and Checkatrade-verified company, the same standards of care that protect your goods in transit apply while they’re in store.
Why People Relocate to Europe — & Why They Choose Wolves

People move to Europe for all sorts of reasons, and we’ve helped families and individuals across each of them. Some are chasing a warmer climate and a slower pace in southern Europe; others relocate for work, study or to be closer to family. Retirees often dream of a second life by the coast, while younger movers take up opportunities in continental cities. Whatever’s prompting your move, the practical challenge is the same — getting your home from one country to another safely, legally and without drama.

That’s exactly what we’re built for. We’re a genuinely family-run business operating from Doryln House, London Road, Ashington, near Pulborough, and we’re fully insured with £10m liability cover. We’re recommended by respected names including Fine & Country, Justin Lloyd and Mansell McTaggart, which reflects the trust local estate agents place in how we handle their clients’ moves. You can learn more about our story and values on our about us page, and when you’re ready to talk specifics, call us on 01903 893731 or email contact@wolves-removals.co.uk.
faqs: Do you handle the customs paperwork for European removals?

We guide you through the documentation a cross-border move typically requires, including the detailed inventory that accompanies any international consignment, and we explain the process in plain English. Since the UK left the EU there are more administrative steps than there once were, so we help you prepare the right forms in good time. We keep our guidance general and practical rather than issuing country-specific rulings — for definitive customs requirements we’ll always direct you to the appropriate authorities.
Should I choose a dedicated load or a part load?

It depends on your volume, budget and how flexible you are on dates. A dedicated load reserves a vehicle exclusively for your belongings and travels directly to your destination, giving the tightest delivery window. A part load shares space with other consignments heading the same way, which keeps costs lower for smaller volumes. We’ll assess your move at survey and recommend whichever genuinely suits you. See our pricing page or request a quote to compare.
How far in advance should I book a European move?

The earlier the better. Early booking secures both your preferred dates and the right vehicle, and it gives us more flexibility around ferry timetables, driving-hours rules and seasonal demand, which peaks over summer and around month-end completions. As soon as your dates begin to firm up, get in touch through our get a quote page so we can build your plan with sensible contingency.
Can you store my belongings if my new home abroad isn’t ready?

Yes. Our secure storage facility at the UK end bridges any gap between moving out and moving in, keeping your belongings safe until your destination is ready. Where it’s needed, we can also arrange holding solutions at the destination through trusted partners, so a mismatch in dates never forces a rushed decision. The same care that protects your goods in transit applies while they’re in store.
